discount_info: threshold authorities, and contract structuring mechanics for managed enterprise networks

entries.text: Commitments beyond stated SLAs (e.g., 99.999% uptime or <2 hour P1 resolution) require explicit Solution Engineering and Deal Desk pricing reviews.

Bespoke integration development (custom middleware for proprietary customer application alarms) is prohibited under the standard Managed Services MSA and requires a separate Professional Services SOW.
The Pricing Catalog defines standard commercial models, OpEx frameworks, billing triggers, and volume thresholds for VI Business (India).
The document is applicable for designing Commercial Assumptions, OpEx matrices, and TCO evaluations in telecom bounds.
Indicative MRC for Edge Broadband (FTTx) is ₹ 8,000 - ₹ 15,000 per month.
Indicative OTC for Edge Broadband (FTTx) is ₹ 15,000.
Indicative MRC for DIA Enterprise Fiber is ₹ 25,000 - ₹ 75,000 per month.
Indicative OTC for DIA Enterprise Fiber is ₹ 50,000.
Indicative MRC for Active 4G/LTE Backup is ₹ 1,500 - ₹ 3,000 per month.
Indicative OTC for Active 4G/LTE Backup is ₹ 5,000 (including router).
Indicative OTC for Core Hub 1 Gbps PtP is ₹ 1,00,000.
Placeholder INR rates represent standard un-discounted rack rates; bid-specific discounting requires systematic Deal Desk margin validation based on deal TCV.
DIA Enterprise Fiber offers bandwidth capacities ranging from 50 Mbps to 500 Mbps.
DIA Enterprise Fiber pricing ranges from ₹ 25,000 to ₹ 75,000 per month.
DIA Enterprise Fiber charges between ₹ 1,500 and ₹ 3,000 for additional services.
DIA Enterprise Fiber includes a router with an indicative one-time charge of ₹ 5,000.
Core Hub has an indicative one-time charge (OTC) of ₹ 1,00,000.
Connectivity Tier pricing includes an indicative one-time charge (OTC) of ₹ 1,50,000+ (varies by distance).
Hyperscaler Interconnect Subscriptions have bandwidth capacities with indicative monthly recurring charges (MRC) and one-time charges (OTC).
Managed SOC and SIEM Licensing pricing scales based on ingestion volume (Events Per Second or daily log GB) and integrated SD-WAN edge points.
Managed SOC and SIEM Licensing prohibits surprise hourly 'time-and-materials' charges for P1 anomalies.
IoT Enterprise Mobility (EMM) offers volume discounts at scale markers: 100k, 500k, 1M, and 2.5M SIMs.
IoT Enterprise Mobility (EMM) prohibits individual endpoint overage penalties unless total organizational data limits are exceeded.
Pricing is illustrative and subject to regional transmission variants.
Monthly billing for an individual location is securely activated 24 hours after validated ZTP configuration loading and formal User Acceptance Sign-off on stability.
Connectivity Tier pricing is illustrative and subject to regional transmission variants; indicative MRC and OTC values are provided as baseline representations.
Reselling of bare-metal AWS/Azure compute VMs (non-networking) is excluded from VI Business core networking solutions and requires separate systems integration partnerships.
SLA financial credits are capped at 10% to 15% of the offending service's MRC, aligning with liability structures in Standard Terms and Conditions.
The Standard Commercial Policy establishes financial guardrails, hardware leasing mechanisms, discount threshold authorities, and contract structuring mechanics for managed enterprise networks.

The policy applies to Deal Desk, Finance Managers, Sales Leads, and Bid Directors for drafting RFP Commercial Assumptions, generating OpEx TCO evaluations, and detailing minimum contract duration lock-ins.
VI Business (India) must standardize on an end-to-end 'as-a-Service' connectivity model for SD-WAN Edge CPE, cellular failover links, and core SIEM ingestion licenses.
VI retains full capital ownership of all provided hardware throughout the MSA term.
For multi-tiered rollups, billing scales linearly as individual branch site batches go live, with sites lacking active WAN delivery remaining dormant and unbilled.

For long-term MSAs extending beyond 36 months, extreme inflationary fluctuations exceeding standard CPI limits require mandatory commercial indexing protection clauses or re-negotiation gates defined heavily within the master agreement.
The enterprise customer explicitly bears direct financial liability for theft, vandalism, or gross negligence resulting in physical destruction of edge CPE units positioned inside their premises.

Massive IoT fleet distributions utilize aggregate Custom APN data pools to shield customers from erratic billing variations. Individual endpoint overages do not trigger penalty rates unless the total accumulated organizational limit reaches 100% capacity.

Network ports terminating at Azure (ExpressRoute) and AWS (Direct Connect) are priced strictly upon the committed bandwidth pipe. The VI Business MRC does not absorb egress data transfer taxes levied individually by the cloud provider against the customer.
Accumulated monthly SLA financial credits crossing all tracked KPI parameters must never exceed 10% to 15% of that specific, offending link/service’s individual MRC.

Sustained non-payment of uncontested invoices exceeding 60-90 days past the due date structurally empowers VI Business (India) to initiate a staged suspension of non-critical data flows, escalating to hard port isolation, prior to formal contract termination.
Legacy third-party telecom termination fees will not be inherently 'bought out' or subtracted from VI Business (India)'s OpEx proposals without exceptional TCV escalations.

Managed Services are subject to a minimum lock-in period of 36 months. Termination for convenience during this period requires the Customer to pay the remaining MRC for the entire unexpired lock-in duration plus unrecovered capital costs.
